treated at home. The patient has had an increase in disorientation, has residual chest 
congestion, decreased appetite, and has remained in bed, immobile, throughout the period 
with pneumonia. While the residual chest congestion and recovery from pneumonia 
alone would not represent a high risk factor, the patient's immobility and increase in 
confusion could create a high probability of a relapse. In this situation, skilled oversight
